description:
Legal record (#20) in a court register/pinqas from a Rabbanite beit din in Cairo (on f. 5r-v of BL OR 6356). In Hebrew. Location: Cairo. Dated 10 Adar 5443 AM, which is 1683 CE. 

(Document #20) Gift deed (שטר מתנה) in which Gentil bt. Avraham ha-Kohen, widow of Shemuʾel Caballero, bequeaths 3,000 medin to her grandson Yaʿaqov b. Yehuda Carmona. The little boy's (קטן) father is still alive but he is listed as an orphan, implying that his mother (Gentil's daughter) was already deceased. Elsewhere in the same court register, document #4, Gentil lends money to the her son-in-law Yehuda over the course of a four-year repayment period. This preexisting financial relationship helps contextualize why Gentil did not choose to leave the funds with the boy's father, but instead R. Yedīdya שיזון (Sezon/Cezon?), probably a Judaeo-Romance surname. Yedīdya agrees to hold onto the funds after Gentil's death and to deliver the sum to Yaʿaqov when he reaches 20 years of age. Below the main entry there is an addendum that modifies the gift deed slightly: Gentil instead sets aside 1,000 medin for her own burial costs and leaves the remaining 2,000 medin for her grandson. MCD.
description_he: רשומה משפטית (#20) בפנקס בית דין רבני בקהיר (BL OR 6356, דפים 5r-v); בעברית; י' אדר ה'תמ"ג (1683 לספירה); שטר מתנה שבו ז'נטיל בת אברהם הכהן, אלמנת שמואל קַבַּיֵּרוֹ, מקנה 3,000 מֶדין לנכדה יעקב בן יהודה קַרְמוֹנָה; ר' ידידיה שיזון מחזיק בכספים עד הגיע יעקב לגיל 20; תוספת: 1,000 מֶדין שמורים לקבורת ז'נטיל ו-2,000 לנכד
